chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P No. D – 1604 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case 1. For hearing of CMA No.1868/2024 (1 Rule 10) 2. For hearing of main case 02.10.2024 M/s Manzoor Hussain N.Larik and Waqar Ali Phulpoto, Advocates for the petitioners Mr. Asfandyar Kharal, AAG Sindh >>>>>>...<<<<<<< Counsel for the petitioners places on record a copy of the order dated 08-08-2023 passed by this Court in C.Ps No.D- 342 and 453 of 2022 and seeks disposal of this petition in the similar terms. The contents of aforesaid order is reproduced below;- “Petitioners are low grade employees working with respondent No.3/Khairpur Medical College District Khairpur under the health department in grade 1 to 4 against different posts. Their case is that they have been rendering services in the College in the said grades on daily wages/contract basis for the last ten years. When the time came for fulfillment of the said posts on regular basis they were ignored. It has been admitted by respondents during the course of hearing that against the subject posts no strict education qualification or expertise of a particular nature are required except formal fitness and suitability. And such criteria in view of long experience of the petitioners with college is largely fulfilled and covered. It is further stated by the officials that there are almost 55 to 57 posts lying vacant in grade 1 to 4 including sweepers and sanitary workers. Against the said posts, the petitioners working, are 32, it has been proposed and agreed by the parties that in terms of standard operating procedures (SOPs) and relevant rules an advertisement would be made in the newspapers inviting applications from the people at large for appointment against those posts.

It is further stated by the officials that there are almost 55 to 57 posts lying vacant in grade 1 to 4 including sweepers and sanitary workers. Against the said posts, the petitioners working, are 32, it has been proposed and agreed by the parties that in terms of standard operating procedures (SOPs) and relevant rules an advertisement would be made in the newspapers inviting applications from the people at large for appointment against those posts. However, the petitioners working in the said grades will be given priority and their services confirmed in consideration of their long experience. This statement, made from 2 the side of the respondents is made part of the court’s order for enforcement. The counsel for the petitioners is satisfied over such arrangement and seeks disposal of these petitions in the above terms. The petitions therefore, are accordingly disposed of in the terms as stated above.” Learned AAG Sindh does not object to such a disposal. Accordingly, the instant petition is also disposed of in the above terms. Judge Judge ARBROHI
